mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in real retail sales (RSAFS) directly captures consumer demand momentum; sustained increases in this flow variable translate into higher expected corporate revenues and earnings, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following positive RSAFS growth months are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 6.5% indicates substantial labor market slack that erodes household income growth and corporate pricing power, triggering equity risk premium expansion and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances when UNRATE > 6.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A rising federal funds rate combined with falling CPI inflation signals a restrictive monetary stance that tightens financial conditions beyond what price stability requires, depressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between the interaction term (FEDFUNDS delta * negative CPIAUCSL delta) and 20-day forward returns is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During post-crisis recoveries, month-over-month industrial production growth (INDPRO) serves as a high-beta signal of capacity utilization rebound; positive surprises in this supply-side proxy generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO surprises during post-crisis recovery periods coincide with positive 5-day SPX returns in fewer than 55% of observations over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter real GDP growth (GDPC1) surprises reflect aggregate demand expansions that equity markets underreact to due to publication lags; this underreaction corrects over the subsequent quarter, producing positive 60-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following above-median GDPC1 surprises are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate below 2.0% suppresses risk-free yields below typical institutional hurdle rates, forcing portfolio rebalancing into equities and compressing equity risk premiums, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 45% of triggered instances when FEDFUNDS < 2.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in housing starts (HOUST) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) identifies a durable goods-and-labor demand loop where construction multipliers amplify income growth; this conjunction generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In months where both HOUST and PAYEMS accelerate, S&P 500 60-day returns are not significantly higher than in months where only one accelerates (difference-of-means t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) falling below 1,000 thousand units signals collapsing residential investment and negative household wealth effects that reduce consumer spending forecasts, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances when HOUST < 1000.
